location.p:
London is the dominant centre for executive search in the UK, accounting for the majority of retained search engagements conducted each year. The city's position as a global financial centre, home to the European headquarters of hundreds of multinational corporations, and a significant hub for technology, media, professional services, and the creative industries means that demand for senior executive talent is consistently higher — and more competitive — than anywhere else in the country. The density of major organisations, the depth of the available talent pool, and the proximity of search firm offices to client organisations all contribute to making London the most active market for senior hiring in the UK.

The London executive talent market is characterised by intense competition for the best candidates. Senior executives in the capital are approached regularly, have well-developed instincts for distinguishing compelling opportunities from noise, and maintain multiple competing offers through much of their careers. This makes the quality of the search firm — their relationships, their ability to present an opportunity compellingly, and their speed of process — more decisive in London than in any other UK market.
location.roles: CEO, CFO, CTO, CMO, General Counsel, Managing Director, Chief People Officer, VP Sales, Head of Strategy, Chief Risk Officer, Chief Investment Officer, Commercial Director
